text:
BILATERAL BREAST MRI WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 39-year-old woman with history of left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, triple-negative, stage IIB (T2N1M0), treated with neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus dose-dense AC followed by weekly paclitaxel, left lumpectomy with axillary lymph node dissection, adjuvant whole breast radiation with regional nodal irradiation, and adjuvant capecitabine plus pembrolizumab maintenance (now completed). Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant confirmed. Annual surveillance MRI of bilateral breasts, approximately 12 months post-lumpectomy. Query local or regional recurrence.

TECHNIQUE: Dynamic contrast-enhanced (DCE) breast MRI performed on a 3.0 Tesla system using a dedicated bilateral breast coil. Pre- and post-contrast sequences obtained utilizing intravenous gadolinium-based contrast (gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg). Sequences include T1-weighted gradient echo (pre- and dynamic post-contrast with subtraction), T2-weighted fat-suppressed, diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with ADC mapping, and STIR sequences. Maximum intensity projection (MIP) reconstructions generated. The exam was performed in the prone position. Background parenchymal enhancement is assessed per standard lexicon criteria.

COMPARISON: Prior bilateral breast MRI (mid-treatment assessment following neoadjuvant ddAC, approximately 18 months prior); diagnostic bilateral mammogram and left breast ultrasound at time of initial diagnosis.

FINDINGS:

LEFT BREAST:
The left breast demonstrates expected post-treatment changes following lumpectomy, axillary dissection, and adjuvant whole breast radiation with regional nodal irradiation. There is architectural distortion and fibroglandular tissue retraction in the left upper outer quadrant centered on the known surgical bed. A metallic clip is identified at the lumpectomy site, producing expected susceptibility artifact on gradient echo sequences. Surrounding the clip, there is an area of linear and irregular non-mass enhancement measuring approximately 1.9 x 1.5 cm in the greatest cross-sectional dimensions, consistent with post-surgical and post-radiation fibrosis/scarring with associated reactive enhancement. This appearance is stable and expected for this stage of post-treatment evolution and does not display the morphologic or kinetic features concerning for recurrence -- specifically, there is no focal nodular or masslike enhancement with rapid early uptake and washout. Adjacent to the scar, there are two small round foci of fat signal on T1 pre-contrast sequences, consistent with evolving fat necrosis (each less than 0.5 cm), a recognized post-radiation sequela. No new or enlarging suspicious enhancement in the remainder of the left breast. Skin thickening over the left breast is present, mild and diffuse, consistent with post-radiation change; no focal skin nodularity or dermal enhancement to suggest skin involvement by tumor.

No suspicious masses in the left lower inner, lower outer, or central/retroareolar regions. The left nipple-areolar complex appears unremarkable without nipple retraction or abnormal enhancement.

Skin and subcutaneous tissues: Mild, diffuse post-radiation skin thickening as noted above. No abnormal subcutaneous nodules.

LEFT AXILLA:
Post-axillary lymph node dissection changes are noted with soft tissue stranding and absence of expected axillary nodal tissue at levels I and II. There are no definite residual or new lymph nodes identified in the left axillary, infraclavicular, or supraclavicular regions to suggest nodal recurrence. Surgical clips are seen in the left axilla consistent with prior ALND. No abnormal enhancement in the left axillary soft tissues.

RIGHT BREAST:
The right breast has heterogeneously dense fibroglandular tissue (ACR category C). Background parenchymal enhancement is moderate and bilateral, symmetric. No suspicious masses, architectural distortion, or areas of abnormal non-mass enhancement identified. No new or suspicious lesions in any quadrant. No grouped or clustered areas of non-mass enhancement. The right nipple-areolar complex is unremarkable. No intramammary, axillary, or internal mammary lymphadenopathy identified on the right.

Skin and subcutaneous tissues: Normal.

RIGHT AXILLA:
No enlarged or morphologically abnormal lymph nodes identified. Normal-appearing level I and II axillary lymph nodes with preserved fatty hila.

INTERNAL MAMMARY REGION (BILATERAL):
No internal mammary lymphadenopathy or abnormal enhancement identified bilaterally.

DIFFUSION-WEIGHTED IMAGING:
No areas of restricted diffusion suspicious for malignancy identified in either breast. The post-lumpectomy scar in the left upper outer quadrant demonstrates low ADC values in keeping with dense fibrosis rather than viable tumor.

IMPRESSION:

1. Left breast: Post-lumpectomy and post-radiation changes in the left upper outer quadrant are stable and expected in appearance, with scar enhancement and evolving fat necrosis consistent with treatment effect. There is no new or suspicious masslike or non-mass enhancement to suggest locoregional recurrence. No concerning changes at the lumpectomy site.

2. Right breast: No suspicious masses or areas of abnormal enhancement. No mammographically or MRI-occult lesions identified in this high-risk patient with known germline BRCA1 mutation.

3. Bilateral axillae and regional lymph node stations: No evidence of nodal recurrence or new lymphadenopathy.

4. Overall: The current study shows no imaging findings to suggest local, regional, or contralateral breast malignancy. The bilateral findings are reassuring in the context of surveillance for a patient with prior left breast TNBC and germline BRCA1 mutation status.
